compiled_state.title: Update project docs to reflect UI/UX reference library infrastructure
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Updates existing project documentation to reflect the new UI/UX reference library infrastructure: CLAUDE.md architecture section, Quick Reference table, and KNOWN-ISSUES.md.

**Why**: Future agents and developers working in this repo need accurate awareness of the new corpus directory, retrieval script, shim, and pre-commit hook so they can use and maintain the reference library correctly.

**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md Architecture section (corpus, ref-query.sh, shim, hook, provenance file, boundary allowlist maintenance); CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table (dso ref-query command); KNOWN-ISSUES.md (_index.yaml regeneration reminder)
- OUT: New documentation files; design documents; changes to plugins/dso/ documentation (handled within Stories 1-6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Architecture section references plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/ (corpus location), plugins/dso/scripts/ref-query.sh (retrieval), dso ref-query shim, check-corpus-schema.sh pre-commit hook, docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml, and notes that plugins/dso/data/** requires boundary allowlist entry maintenance
  ← Satisfies: Documentation completeness for future agents

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table includes a dso ref-query entry with its flags (--top-n, --tier, --session-hash)
  ← Satisfies: Discoverability for agents using quick reference

- When this story is complete, KNOWN-ISSUES.md has an entry noting that after adding new corpus domain files, _index.yaml must be manually updated and committed (preventing silent manifest drift)
  ← Satisfies: Operational knowledge for corpus maintainers

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting, structure, and conventions

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.
comments.author: Claude
